policies.name: Produce a design doc or ADR before writing code on greenfield work
policies.description: On greenfield work, capture the design and trade-offs in a document or ADR before implementation so the structure is reviewed before code exists.

policies.name: Identify vulnerabilities before trusting code, flagging CWE and OWASP categories
policies.description: Treat code as untrusted until reviewed for vulnerabilities; name the CWE and OWASP Top 10 categories at issue and escalate decisions that need human review.

policies.name: Verify the implementation works for real users with risk-based, evidence-backed testing
policies.description: Design test strategies that cover acceptance criteria and edge cases, prioritized by risk, and report results with evidence rather than asserting success.

policies.name: Keep CI/CD pipelines reproducible, least-privilege, and locally runnable before merge
policies.description: Design pipelines that run the same locally and in CI, pin actions to SHAs, request least privilege, and exercise changes locally before merge.

policies.name: Investigate systematically and rank hypotheses by evidence before issuing a verdict
policies.description: Gather evidence, enumerate and rank hypotheses by likelihood, and only then issue a verdict; do not conclude before the investigation supports it.

policies.name: Write tests alongside code and commit atomically
policies.description: Ship tests in the same change as the code they cover and keep commits atomic so each unit of work is independently reviewable.

policies.name: Spike a throwaway prototype to reduce uncertainty before committing to a design
policies.description: When the problem is poorly understood, write a throwaway spike to learn the constraints before committing to a design, instead of designing on paper first.

policies.name: Delegate specialized work to the right agent and keep a traceable call graph
policies.description: Route each unit of work to the agent specialized for it and record the delegation graph so the session remains auditable.

policies.name: Challenge plans adversarially with evidence and block on unmitigated risk
policies.description: Stress-test plans against checklists and evidence, surface ambiguity and untested claims, and withhold approval while a material risk is unmitigated.

policies.name: Turn each session's experience into durable, classified learnings
policies.description: Run a structured retrospective that gathers data, generates insights, and extracts classified learnings into institutional memory.

policies.name: Prioritize by user outcome using RICE and KANO and guard against strategic drift
policies.description: Frame work as measurable user outcomes, prioritize with RICE and KANO, and flag scope that drifts from the strategic intent.

policies.name: Retrieve relevant memory before reasoning and persist progress at milestones
policies.description: Load task-relevant memories before reasoning and write progress back at milestones so context survives across sessions.

policies.name: Keep each skill atomic: one skill, one task, deduplicated
policies.description: Curate skills so each does one task, reject duplicates, and keep skill indices accurate and reproducible.

policies.name: Write documentation a junior developer can follow without questions
policies.description: Write specs and docs in explicit, unambiguous language with INVEST-shaped stories and testable acceptance criteria.

policies.name: Generate sized, actionable backlog tasks from current project state
policies.description: When capacity is idle, analyze open issues, PRs, and code health and produce a small set of sized, actionable tasks rather than vague intentions.

policies.name: Confirm the root cause before proposing a fix
policies.description: Investigate until the root cause is confirmed; do not propose or apply a fix while the cause is still a guess.

policies.name: Deliver a single prioritized verdict, not a menu of options
policies.description: Cut through ambiguity with one ranked recommendation and the reasoning behind it, rather than handing back a list of options to choose from.

policies.name: Challenge the consensus with evidence and declare uncertainty rather than guess
policies.description: Present the opposing view backed by evidence and cited sources, and state uncertainty explicitly instead of guessing to agree.

policies.name: Review feature requests with constructive skepticism, weighing impact against cost
policies.description: Summarize the ask, weigh user impact against implementation cost, flag unknowns, and give an actionable recommendation rather than rubber-stamping.

policies.name: Remove dead code and orphaned artifacts rather than leaving them to rot
policies.description: Delete unreachable code, stale files, and orphaned references on contact instead of normalizing decay.

policies.name: Resolve merge conflicts from commit intent, never by blind whole-file choice
policies.description: Analyze git history and commit intent to resolve conflicts; never resolve a conflict by taking one whole side without understanding both.

policies.name: Sequence work packages by dependency with explicit acceptance criteria
policies.description: Translate epics into work packages ordered by dependency, each with clear acceptance criteria and documented risks.

policies.name: Resolve competing positions by surfacing underlying interests
policies.description: Find the interests behind competing positions and build a resolution on them, rather than splitting the difference.

policies.name: Acknowledge and resolve every reviewer comment, tracking each to a resolution
policies.description: Gather full comment context, acknowledge every piece of feedback, and map each comment to a resolution status before closing the review.

policies.name: Decompose work into atomic, estimable items with clear done definitions
policies.description: Break PRDs and epics into atomic work items, each estimable and carrying an explicit definition of done.

policies.name: Complete the three mandatory session phases: init, task execution, finalization
policies.description: Every session runs session initialization, per-task execution, and session finalization; skipping a phase leaves the session incomplete.

policies.name: Use conventional commit messages that reference an issue and stay atomic
policies.description: Commit messages follow the conventional format, reference an issue, and each commit touches at most five files.

policies.name: Prefer an existing skill over raw gh, git, or shell commands
policies.description: Check for a tested skill before running raw commands; if a capability is missing, extend the skill rather than scripting inline.

policies.name: Read project constraints, ADRs, and memories before relying on pre-training
policies.description: Prefer retrieval over pre-training: read PROJECT-CONSTRAINTS, ADRs, and memories before making decisions they govern.

policies.name: Run linting and validation scoped to changed files before opening a PR
policies.description: Before opening a PR, run markdownlint and validation scripts scoped to changed files so feedback arrives locally instead of in CI.
